rulings.Case Title: RICHARD LAU VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Writ Of Mandate As To Respondent California Dmv
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 4, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER RICHARD LAU's Petition for Writ Of Mandate As To Respondent California DMV. Petitioner Richard Lau's petition for writ of mandate from the suspension of his driving privilege is denied. Mr. Lau fails to show that respondent DMV abused its discretion or its findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence. The DMV properly admitted the DS-367 officer's statement under Evidence Code 1280 as a public employee record. (Jackson v. Department of Motor Vehicles (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 730, 737.) The failure of Officer McFall to sign the first page of the officer's statement that referred to objective symptoms of intoxication does not render the statement untrustworthy and inadmissible under Evidence Code 1280. The presumption that Officer McFall regularly performed his duty per Evidence Code 664 is unrebutted and other portions of the officer's statement clearly show that Officer McFall observed objective indicia of Mr. Lau's intoxication. (AR 13.) The weight of the evidence supports the DMV's finding that Mr. Lau was driving. The DMV properly admitted Ranger Mitra's observations of driving. Mr. Lau admitted that he had been drinking and was trying to go home when he spoke with Officer McFall. (AR 13; see also AR 20.) These statements are admissible as party admissions. Ranger Mitra's statements about Mr. Lau are admissible under Government Code 11513(d) to supplement or explain Mr. Lau's admissions. (Lake v. Reed (1997) 16 Cal.4th 448, 461-462.) The DMV established that Mr. Lau's blood-alcohol content was at .08% or higher based on the officer's statement, the recorded test results, and the presumption of Evidence Code 664. (Taxara v. Gutierrez (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 945, 949.) Mr. Lau did not produce any affirmative evidence that casts doubt on these facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the DMV is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY, AS SUBROGEE TO VS. TRITON SUBS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Triton Subs, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set Seven Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Triton Subs, Inc., In The Amount Of 1,050.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 4, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY, QFA ROYALTIES LLC, QUIZNOS FRANCHISING II, LLC, AND QIP HOLDER, LLC's Motion To Compel Defendant Triton Subs, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set Seven Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Triton Subs, Inc., In The Amount Of 1,050.00. Plaintiff Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to compel defendant Triton Subs, Inc. to provide further responses to special interrogatories (set seven) and for monetary sanctions is granted. Triton's objections to special interrogatories 72-76 are stricken and it must provide further verified responses to those interrogatories by providing all non-privileged responsive information in its custody, possession and control and, if it is withholding any information based on an assertion of a privilege, it must identify the basis for the asserted privilege with sufficient particularity to enable Fireman's Fund to evaluate and challenge the assertion of a privilege. Triton must provide a further verified response without objection to special interrogatory 78. Triton must pay sanctions of $1000 to Fireman's Fund. The further responses and payment of sanctions must occur by June 15, 2018. The court adopts the reasoning set forth in the report of Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Fireman's Fund is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches the report of Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOGICAL IDEAS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. COASTAL VENTURES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiff Logical Ideas, Incs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 4, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF LOGICAL IDEAS, INC.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff Logical Ideas, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted as to defendants Coastal Ventures, Inc. and Richard Chiang and the motion and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied as to defendant James Olsen. Coastal and Mr. Chiang did not oppose the motion. There are triable disputes whether Mr. Olsen is a partner, alter ego or otherwise responsible for the debts of Coastal. Mr. Olsen is not bound by Mr. Chiang's and Coastal's admissions. (CCP 2033.410(b)). Mr. Olsen presents admissible evidence that he was an independent contractor working for Coastal, did not pay and was not responsible for its debts, there was no partnership and he is not a partner of Coastal. (Olsen Depo. 83:25-84:20, 104:7-25, 108:7-109:8; Olsen Dec. pars. 3-9; Chiang Dec. pars. 4 and 7-9). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Mr. Olsen is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO ARTIGA VS. HAWK LING LOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To (1) Compel Further Responses From Defendant Tom Jue To Plaintiffs Demands For Production Of Documents, Set One (2) Compel Compliance With Court Order (3) Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 4, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF FERNANDO ARTIGA, FERNANDO ARTIGA's Motion To (1) Compel Further Responses From Defendant Tom Jue To Plaintiffs Demands For Production Of Documents, Set One (2) Compel Compliance With Court Order (3) Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Stephen Zollman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' motion GRANTED in full. Parties are ordered to check in with the clerk, meet and confer as to what discovery remains outstanding and then report back to court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to szollman1@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SUCCESSOR AGENCY TO THE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY OF VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUSEUM OF MODERN ART ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 4, 2018, Line 7, CROSS-DEFENDANT DEGENKOLB ENGINEERS' DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant Degenkolb Engineers' demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by Safway Services, LLC is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Degenkolb's certificate of merit does not comply with the requirement that the person consulted must render an opinion whether Degenkolb was or was not negligent in performing its services. Safway has leave to correct this deficiency if it can do so in good faith. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WONDWOSSEN MEKBEB VS. CLARENCE LALAIND JR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 4, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Wondwossen Mekbeb is granted without leave to amend, but without prejudice to Mr. Mekbeb seeking leave to amend if and when he is able to allege facts that are sufficient to impose punitive damages liability on Uber per Civil Code 3294(b). Accepting the allegation that Uber is the employer of defendant Clarence Lalaind, Jr. as true, the FAC fails to allege sufficient facts to show any of the grounds on which an employer may be liable for punitive damages. Nor does it appear that it is reasonably possible for Mr. Mekbeb to allege such facts at this stage of the case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ARYAN DAVANI VS. E-ZASSI, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 4, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANTS JENNIFER CREECH, E-ZASSI, LLC, AND EZASSI, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens. Defendants' motion to dismiss for forum non conveniens is continued to June 11, 2018 to give defendants an opportunity to consider whether they will agree that, if this case is litigated in Florida that California law, not Florida law, will govern the claims of plaintiff Aryan Davani and defendants' defenses, including California law regarding statute of limitations and exceptions thereto. If defendants so agree, they must file a document so stating by June 7, 2018. If defendants so agree, the court will stay this case pending the conclusion of the Florida case. Absent such an agreement, the mandatory forum selection clause is unreasonable and unenforceable because Mr. Davani would not be able to litigate his claims in Florida on their merits. Labor Code 925 is inapplicable to the parties' agreement because the agreement was executed before January 1, 2017, its terms preclude oral modification, there was no consideration supporting the purported oral modification, and Mr. Davani never fulfilled the terms of the purported oral modification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT KOPF VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Stay The Case Based On Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 4, 2018, Line 12, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Stay The Case Based On Forum Non Conveniens. Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion to dismiss or stay based on forum non conveniens is denied. A corporate defendant's principal place of business is presumptively a convenient forum. (Nat'l Football League v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (2013) 216 Cal. App. 4th 902, 917.) Where the plaintiff is a California resident, the plaintiff's choice of a forum should rarely be disturbed unless the balance of public and private interest is strongly in favor of the defendant. (Id.) Uber, a California corporate resident, has failed to show that equity strongly favors requiring plaintiff Robert Kopf, a California resident, to litigate his claims in New York. While the incident occurred in New York and potential police witnesses presumably reside in New York, Uber failed to present evidence that the Uber driver and the bystanders who witnessed the incident at an international airport are also residents of New York. The cases relied on by Uber where courts granted a forum non conveniens motion are distinguishable because those cases involved a plaintiff who did not reside in California, but rather resided in the state where the incident occurred. Because Mr. Kopf is a California resident, California has a strong interest in having this case adjudicated in this state. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Kopf is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL GOMES VS. FRANK CHU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 4, 2018, Line 14, DEFENDANT FRANK CHU'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Frank Chu's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Daniel Gomes is denied. The allegation that Mr. Chu knowingly drove a vehicle while he was intoxicated is sufficient to show malice for purposes of Civil Code 3294(c)(1) and no case construing that subsection holds to the contrary.
